description: §61.7 — the `method=` value may be a bareword (unquoted) HTTP method literal, and GET (a non-POST method) is in the recognized set. A `method=GET` bareword endpoint, exhaustive over its enum, gates clean — no E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID.
rationale: §61.7: `method=` SHALL be one of GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE, accepted as a quoted string-literal OR a bareword. A bareword GET is recognized, so E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID stays silent and the family is clean.
